channel.title: java8 on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts
channel.description: Recent content in java8 on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts

item.title: Let's create coffee with a decorator pattern with the help of lambda expression
item.description: java.util.function.Function<T, R> is a functional interface. It takes a type T and returns type R. We mostly use it to transform a type into a different one. But it doesn’t end there. We can use this into a lambda expression to change a value as well. For example- Function<Integer, Integer> doubleIt = a -> a * 2; Over here, we are receiving and in integer but returning after doubling it. Thus, we can pass this function as an argument of a method as well.
